title: Fix crash when GUI stderr logging fails
description: Opening images can wake background MCP/single-instance paths in the packaged GUI. Crash report from 2026-05-17 shows eprintln! in the MCP socket task panicking, followed by the panic hook eprintln! panicking again, aborting the app. Make GUI/background diagnostic logging non-fatal and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Fixed GUI/background diagnostic logging so stderr write failures no longer panic or abort the app; added regression coverage and verified the MCP socket tests in a clean worktree.

title: Model download: add pause, cancel, and restart controls
description: Complete the remaining model-download UX after the backend atomic/resumable download slice. Add pause/resume, cancel, restart/retry controls in the Embedding Explorer and JobProgressPanel, and wire them to cancellable backend job state instead of a single blocking invoke.
acceptance_criteria: User can pause/resume an in-progress CLIP model download; user can cancel without creating the final model file; user can restart/retry after a failed or cancelled download; partial .part files are reused only when resuming.
close_reason: Added job-backed pause/resume/cancel controls for CLIP model downloads, retry/resume UI after cancellation or failure, resumable .part handling, and regression tests for paused/cancelled streams.

title: Audit P0: stop returning stored API keys to the renderer
description: The audit found get_api_key returns raw keychain secrets through Tauri IPC. Renderer code should use has/set/delete/validate flows without retrieving full secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend API no longer exposes getApiKey; production Tauri command registration no longer exposes get_api_key; settings continue to show key presence via hasApiKey.
close_reason: Implemented audit P0 fixes: CSP/narrow static asset scope with runtime library-root scope, non-destructive health default, safe DB recovery language, removed renderer API key readback, and registered/tested rescan_sidecars contract.

title: Audit P0: fix frontend/backend command contract mismatch for rescan_sidecars
description: The audit found src/lib/api.ts invokes rescan_sidecars while command registration is commented out. Broken invokes should be caught by tests or fixed.
acceptance_criteria: rescan_sidecars is registered or the frontend wrapper is removed; a command contract test covers frontend invoke names against Tauri registrations.
close_reason: Implemented audit P0 fixes: CSP/narrow static asset scope with runtime library-root scope, non-destructive health default, safe DB recovery language, removed renderer API key readback, and registered/tested rescan_sidecars contract.

title: Audit P0: make startup library health check non-destructive
description: The audit found startup checkLibraryHealth can purge image records because auto_purge_missing defaults to true. Missing files should be marked unavailable, not deleted, unless a user explicitly confirms purge.
acceptance_criteria: auto_purge_missing defaults false; startup health check never deletes image DB rows by default; UI setting reflects the safer default.
close_reason: Implemented audit P0 fixes: CSP/narrow static asset scope with runtime library-root scope, non-destructive health default, safe DB recovery language, removed renderer API key readback, and registered/tested rescan_sidecars contract.

title: Audit P0: remove destructive database recovery guidance
description: The audit found startup DB open failures tell the user to delete cull.db. That conflicts with the data-safety rule that cull.db contains real user data.
acceptance_criteria: DB error dialog no longer recommends deleting or resetting cull.db; message points to backup/repair/safe diagnostics instead.
close_reason: Implemented audit P0 fixes: CSP/narrow static asset scope with runtime library-root scope, non-destructive health default, safe DB recovery language, removed renderer API key readback, and registered/tested rescan_sidecars contract.

title: Audit P0: lock down Tauri asset protocol and CSP
description: The audit found csp:null and assetProtocol.scope.allow containing '**', which can expose arbitrary local files if the renderer is compromised or path-bearing content is injected. Narrow asset scope to app-owned/authorized image roots and add a real CSP.
acceptance_criteria: No wildcard asset protocol scope remains; CSP is non-null and blocks remote/inline scripts by default; asset protocol config has a regression test.
close_reason: Implemented audit P0 fixes: CSP/narrow static asset scope with runtime library-root scope, non-destructive health default, safe DB recovery language, removed renderer API key readback, and registered/tested rescan_sidecars contract.

title: Loupe image asset fallback
description: Loupe can show a broken image icon when the full-size source asset URL fails even though the source file and generated thumbnail exist. Add a frontend fallback from the full source path to the app-managed thumbnail so loupe mode always displays an image when a thumbnail is available.
close_reason: Fixed loupe full-size image load fallback to generated thumbnails, with unit coverage.

title: P0: Broad format support — add HEIC, TIFF, BMP, SVG, AVIF, JPEG XL, RAW (CR2/NEF/ARW/DNG) via image crate features + platform decoders
P0: Native macOS menu bar — File > Open, Open Folder, recent files, Edit, View, Window, Help menus
P0: Drag and drop from Finder — handle Tauri file-drop events to import/open images dropped onto app window
P0: File type associations — register CFBundleDocumentTypes for all image UTTypes via Tauri fileAssociations config so app appears in Finder 'Open With' menu
Google Gemini Embedding 2 — 3072-dim cross-modal embeddings via API
description:
Google Gemini Embedding 2 — highest quality cross-modal embeddings.

## Specs
- 3072 dimensions (truncatable to 768/1536)  
- Cross-modal: text + image + video + audio in same vector space
- GA released 2025, production-ready
- Requires Google API key

## API
- Endpoint: Vertex AI or Gemini API
- Send base64 image → get 3072-dim float vector
- Rate limits: check current quotas

## Implementation
- Add as EmbeddingProvider in the pluggable system
- Store API key in app settings (encrypted)
- Settings panel: Google API Key field
- Batch processing with rate limiting
- Falls back to local CLIP if no API key

## Why Priority
- Best embedding quality available
- Text-image search in same space (search images by text description)
- No local model download needed (API-based)
- 3072-dim gives much better clustering than CLIP 512-dim

## DB Impact
- embeddings table already supports variable dims
- 3072 x 4 bytes = 12KB per image embedding
- 10K images = ~120MB of embedding data

title: Embedding Explorer: show thumbnails at zoom, not just dots
Model download: progress bar, pause/resume/restart, manual download option
description:
Model download needs proper UX:

## Current Problem
- Download is a blind blocking call with no progress indication
- User has no idea how much is downloaded or remaining
- No way to cancel, pause, or resume
- No manual download option

## Requirements
- Progress bar showing downloaded MB / total MB + percentage
- Estimated time remaining
- Pause / Resume button
- Cancel / Restart button
- Manual download option: show URL + expected file path so user can download with curl/browser
- Handle network errors gracefully with retry

## Implementation
- Use reqwest streaming (not blocking) with content-length header
- Emit Tauri events with download progress (bytes_downloaded, total_bytes)
- Frontend shows progress bar in the Embedding Explorer left panel
- Store partial downloads for resume (HTTP Range header)
close_reason: Completed model download UX: streaming progress with bytes and ETA, pause/resume/cancel/retry controls, resumable .part downloads, manual curl command with actual model path, and backend/frontend regression tests.

comments.author: glebis
comments.text: Backend safety slice completed: CLIP model downloads now stream to clip-vit-b32-vision.onnx.part, resume via HTTP Range when supported, and atomically rename into place only after the stream completes. Remaining pause/cancel/restart UX is tracked by imageview-3lr.1.

title: Multi-model embedding support — pluggable providers including Google, Ollama, OpenAI, local ONNX
description:
Support multiple embedding models via a pluggable provider system.

## Architecture
EmbeddingProvider trait in Rust with implementations for each backend:

trait EmbeddingProvider {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn dimensions(&self) -> usize;
    fn generate(&self, image_path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<f32>>;
    fn is_available(&self) -> bool;
}

## Providers (priority order)
1. **CLIP ViT-B/32 (ONNX local)** — current implementation, 512-dim, works offline
2. **Google Multimodal Embedding** — via Vertex AI or Gemini API, 1408-dim, best quality
3. **Ollama** — local models like llava, bakllava, moondream via ollama API (http://localhost:11434)
4. **OpenAI CLIP** — via API, requires key
5. **Nomic Embed Vision** — via Ollama or API, 768-dim
6. **Custom ONNX** — user provides their own .onnx file

## DB Changes
- embeddings table already has model_name column — each provider stores under its name
- User can have embeddings from multiple models simultaneously
- Embedding Explorer lets you pick which model's embeddings to visualize

## UI
- Settings/preferences for embedding provider selection
- Sidebar in Embedding Explorer shows model dropdown
- 'Generate Embeddings' button uses the currently selected provider
- Show which images have/don't have embeddings for current model

## Ollama Integration
- Check if Ollama is running: GET http://localhost:11434/api/tags
- List available vision models
- Use /api/embeddings endpoint with base64 image
- Models: llava, bakllava, moondream, llava-llama3

## Google Multimodal
- Use Gemini API (gemini-2.0-flash or multimodalembedding@001)
- Requires API key (stored in app settings)
- Best quality embeddings but requires network

title: Embedding Explorer view — UMAP scatter + hierarchical cluster tree
CLIP embedding generation via ONNX Runtime — background processing on import
URL protocol scheme for agent-driven launch with precise parameters
description:
Agents must be able to launch ImageView via URL protocol with precise parameters.

## URL Protocol Examples

imageview://open?path=/path/to/image.png&zoom=200&view=loupe
imageview://open?folder=/path/to/folder&view=grid&size=160
imageview://compare?ids=img-1,img-2,img-3
imageview://open?paths=/a.png,/b.png,/c.png&view=grid&focus=1
imageview://open?folder=/path&filter=min_size:256&sort=rating

## Parameters
- path: single image path
- paths: comma-separated image paths
- folder: folder to import/open
- view: grid|compare|loupe|canvas|lineage|embeddings
- zoom: zoom percentage (100=fit, 200=2x, etc.)
- size: thumbnail size in grid view (px)
- focus: index of image to focus (0-based)
- filter: query string for filtering (min_size, rating, format, etc.)
- sort: sort order (date, name, rating, size)
- position: window position x,y
- fullscreen: true/false

## Implementation
1. Register imageview:// URL scheme in tauri.conf.json (deep linking plugin)
2. Parse URL parameters in Rust setup handler
3. Apply parameters to app state on launch
4. If app already running, handle as IPC message (single instance)
5. CLI should also accept same parameters: imageview open --path /a.png --zoom 200

## Also needed for MCP
The MCP server should be able to trigger these same actions programmatically.

title: Accept/reject sends wrong values - frontend uses 'accepted'/'rejected' but DB expects 'accept'/'reject'
Command palette: add navigation and collection result providers
description: Add dynamic palette providers for opening app views, jumping to folders, manual collections, smart collections, sessions, canvases, detected classes, and current import batches. These should rank contextually and execute through existing store/navigation/image-loading paths.\n\nThis task should preserve the existing natural-language search flow in CommandBar.svelte while making saved smart collections and navigation destinations discoverable from the palette.
acceptance_criteria: Users can use the palette to switch views and jump to folders, collections, smart collections, sessions/canvases where available; result rows include counts/context where cheap to compute; selecting a destination clears conflicting active scopes correctly and reloads images as the sidebar handlers do.

title: Command palette: build accessible palette UI shell
description: Build the Svelte command palette overlay/modal that opens from the global command shortcuts and queries the shared command registry. The UI should use Cull's Tokyo Night tokens, monospace style, compact rows, no decorative cards, and support command-mode prefixes.\n\nBaseline interactions: fuzzy search, arrow navigation, Enter execute, Escape close, visible shortcut badges, category/context labels, loading/empty/error states, and no interference with normal text input shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Palette opens with Cmd+K and command-only mode can open with Shift+Cmd+P; keyboard interaction works without mouse; rows show title/category/shortcut/context; ARIA combobox/listbox semantics or an equivalent accessible pattern are implemented; no hardcoded colors outside design tokens.

title: Command palette: implement shared command registry
description: Create a typed frontend command registry with stable command IDs, titles, categories, keywords, default keybindings, optional when/context predicates, and async run handlers. The registry should become the source of truth for palette rows and for migrated keyboard/menu actions.\n\nThe command context should expose current view, focused image, selected images, active folder/collection/smart collection/session/canvas, loaded collections, feature availability, and safe helpers for reload/toast/navigation.
acceptance_criteria: At least the existing view switching, sidebar toggle, undo/redo, trash, rating/decision, collect mode, settings, import, and search-open actions are represented as command definitions or explicitly deferred with reasons; unit tests cover filtering by context and duplicate keybinding detection.

title: Command palette: audit command surfaces and shortcut strategy
description: Audit current command surfaces in src/lib/keys.ts, src/lib/menu.ts, context menus, CommandBar.svelte, Sidebar.svelte, and Tauri API calls. Produce a concrete registry contract, command categories, context model, shortcut policy, and migration plan.\n\nInclude a macOS shortcut decision: default Cmd+K universal palette, Shift+Cmd+P command-only mode, keep / and Cmd+F for image search, and document whether Cmd+P is offered only as an optional Obsidian preset because it conflicts with Print.
acceptance_criteria: Registry contract and shortcut policy are documented in the issue/design notes; command categories and context inputs are explicit; migration list covers keys.ts, menu.ts, context menu actions, search, collections, and AI/batch actions.

title: Command palette and workflow launcher
description:
Build a command palette for Cull that unifies keyboard shortcuts, menu actions, navigation, collection workflows, and future saved automations. The palette should follow proven patterns from Obsidian, VS Code, Raycast, and GitHub while fitting Cull's keyboard-first image curation workflow.

Core direction: create a shared command registry first, then have the palette, global shortcuts, app menus, context menus, and later CLI/MCP surfaces consume the same command definitions. Avoid a standalone modal that duplicates behavior from keys.ts and menu.ts.

Research constraints:
- Default shortcut should not be Cmd+P only; Cmd+P is Print on macOS and Quick Open in VS Code. Prefer Cmd+K for universal palette, with Shift+Cmd+P as command-only mode and an optional Obsidian-style preset.
- Keep existing / and Cmd+F behavior for natural-language image search.
- Pinning should cover commands, searches, and workflows, and should not be confused with the existing pinned import target.
- User-definable automations should be saved sequences of known Cull commands before considering arbitrary scripts.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when users can open a fast keyboard-first palette, execute context-aware Cull commands, see shortcuts, pin frequent items, use recent/frequent ranking, and run at least one saved workflow built from registered commands.

title: First-class image tags and enrichment backfill
description: Add persistent image tags and a backfill/enrichment path that promotes existing vision metadata, object detections, source labels, generation metadata, and file facts into searchable/filterable/exportable tags.
close_reason: Implemented persistent image tag tables, enrichment backfill from existing metadata/detections/source/generation/file facts, tag-aware smart collections/search, export manifest tags, Tauri/API commands, and tests.

title: Similar-image group persistence from embeddings
description: Persist generated similar-image groups from existing embedding vectors so scene/group workflows can be queried and exported.
acceptance_criteria: Backend can generate groups for an embedding model, list group summaries, and list images in a group through Tauri/API commands.
close_reason: Implemented greedy threshold embedding grouping with durable group tables, list/group image commands, TypeScript API wrappers, and Rust tests.

title: Image quality metrics: blur, focus, exposure
description: Compute deterministic local quality metrics for imported images: blur/focus via Laplacian-style edge energy and exposure via luminance histogram/clipping. Persist per image with analyzer version.
acceptance_criteria: Backend can analyze selected image IDs, store typed metrics, return metrics by image ID, and filter smart collections by blur/focus/exposure fields.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic local image quality metrics with schema, analyzer, Tauri/API commands, smart collection fields, and tests.

title: Curation automation: quality, grouping, delivery sets
description: Track the feasible curation features identified in research: deterministic image quality metrics, similar-image grouping, best-of-group ranking, smart collection filters, and delivery/contact sheet workflows.
acceptance_criteria: High-feasibility slices are decomposed into child tasks; implemented slices have tests and are wired through Tauri/API boundaries.

title: Audit P1: add migration versioning and backup-before-migration
description: The audit found migrations run as an ad-hoc chain without schema_migrations/user_version tracking or backup before mutating the real user database.
acceptance_criteria: DB open records applied migrations, rejects unknown future schema versions, runs integrity check, and backs up cull.db before migrations that alter schema/data.
close_reason: Added migration preflight, schema_migrations/user_version tracking, integrity check, future-version rejection, and pre-migration SQLite backup coverage.

title: Audit P1: replace unbounded embedding APIs with paged/vector-indexed flows
description: The audit found get_all_embeddings and similarity search load all vectors, copy them over IPC, and sort all scores. This will fail at large library sizes.
acceptance_criteria: Default UI paths do not request all embeddings over IPC; similarity/projection flows are paged, sampled, cached, or indexed; worker payloads use typed transferable buffers where applicable.
close_reason: Replaced renderer-facing all-embedding IPC with paged flat embedding payloads; Embedding Explorer now projects a capped page with transferable Float32Array worker payloads; similarity lookup no longer materializes all embeddings first.

title: Audit P1: canonicalize MCP scope checks and redact audit params
description: The audit found MCP path scope checks use uncanonicalized starts_with and audit logging stores raw params_json, including potentially sensitive paths/prompts.
acceptance_criteria: MCP folder/file scope checks compare canonical paths and do not allow parent traversal; stored mcp_audit_log params are redacted or summarized by default.
close_reason: Implemented canonicalized MCP scope checks and default audit parameter redaction; tests added and passing.

title: Implement Hugging Face model runner profiles and exportable pipelines
description: Follow-up to the initial common model processing pipeline and Hugging Face API key onboarding. Add direct Hugging Face model execution as a first-class provider profile, including model selection, task mapping, model_runs/model_run_items provenance, remote transport audit logging, cancellation/progress, and exportable production pipeline definitions.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create/select a Hugging Face model profile; a model run can execute against Hugging Face for at least one image task; results are recorded in model_runs/model_run_items; remote calls appear in privacy/audit surfaces; a saved pipeline definition can be exported.

title: Apple Photos/iCloud import via PhotoKit catalog connector
description: Build a macOS-only Apple Photos import source that uses PhotoKit to read the user's System Photo Library, including iCloud-backed assets, then materializes selected assets into Cull's existing file import pipeline. Preserve Apple Photos provenance and mirror selected albums into Cull collections. Research is captured in docs/2026-05-13-icloud-photos-import-research.md.
acceptance_criteria: NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription is present; Tauri commands expose PhotoKit authorization status/request; app can list albums and image assets from the System Photo Library; importing selected assets copies original/current versions into Cull-controlled storage and runs existing import logic; Apple asset/album provenance is persisted; selected Photos albums can become Cull collections; iCloud-only assets show download progress/cancel/error states.

title: P1: Histogram display — live histogram overlay in Loupe view, per-channel RGB, over/underexposure warnings
P1: Batch operations pipeline — composable action sequences: resize, convert, rename, watermark, export — triggered via CLI, URL scheme, or GUI
P1: URL scheme expansion — extend imageview:// to support action verbs: import, export, search, contact-sheet, resize, with full parameter vocabulary
P1: Share sheet integration — outbound NSSharingServicePicker to share images from ImageView to other apps
P1: Reveal in Finder — command to show selected image's location in Finder
P1: Quick Look extension — QLPreviewingController appex so Spacebar preview in Finder uses ImageView rendering
P1: EXIF/metadata display panel — read and display EXIF, IPTC, XMP data in sidebar or overlay; sortable in grid view
Accept folder path as CLI argument — imageview /path/to/folder opens and imports immediately
AI generation metadata parsing — extract prompt/seed/model from PNG/EXIF
description:
Parse AI generation parameters from PNG/EXIF on import. No ML needed.

## Schema: image_generation_metadata table
- Columns: image_id, source, parser, prompt, negative_prompt, seed, steps, sampler, model, cfg_scale, raw_json
- FTS5 virtual table for prompt full-text search
- ~1ms per image (metadata only, no pixel decode)

## Crates
- png — PNG text chunks/iTXt parsing (Stable Diffusion, ComfyUI, A1111)
- kamadak-exif — EXIF from JPEG/WebP (Midjourney, DALL-E)
- serde_json — ComfyUI workflow JSON parsing

## Supported Formats
- Stable Diffusion A1111: 'parameters' text chunk with 'Prompt: ... Steps: ... Seed: ...'
- ComfyUI: 'workflow' and 'prompt' JSON in PNG text chunks
- Midjourney: Description EXIF field
- DALL-E: XMP metadata

## UI
- Show prompt in Loupe info panel
- Search by prompt text (FTS5)
- Filter by model, sampler
- Group by seed (find all variations of same prompt)

title: Perceptual hashing (pHash) for near-duplicate detection
description:
Compute perceptual hash on import for near-duplicate detection.

## Schema: image_perceptual_hashes table  
- Columns: image_id, algorithm, hash_hi (i64), hash_lo (i64), band0-3 (i64 for LSH)
- Band indexes for candidate filtering, Hamming distance in Rust
- ~5ms per image after decode

## Crate
- img_hash — configurable pHash/dHash/aHash

## Near-Duplicate Detection
- On import: compute hash, check bands against existing hashes
- If Hamming distance < threshold → flag as near-duplicate
- Show duplicate groups in sidebar
- Option to auto-skip duplicates on import
close_reason: Implemented schema v18 image_perceptual_hashes, DCT pHash generation on import/backfill, band-indexed near-duplicate lookup, Tauri/API commands, mocks, and Rust tests.

title: Florence-2 integration — API (HuggingFace/Ollama) and local ONNX for zero-shot detection + captioning
Research: additional embedding/metadata sources for richer image understanding
description:
Rich metadata sources for image understanding. Research complete.

## Tier 1 — High impact, easy to add
- **Color analysis** (k-means palette, no ML) — instant, cluster by color
- **Perceptual hashing** (pHash/dHash) — near-duplicate detection
- **PNG/EXIF metadata parsing** — extract AI generation params (prompt, seed, model, steps)
- **Aesthetic scoring** (CLIP-based, HPSv2) — rate image quality 1-10

## Tier 2 — High impact, needs model download
- **DINOv2** (300MB ONNX) — better embeddings than CLIP for visual similarity
- **SigLIP 2** (ONNX) — multilingual image-text search, better zero-shot than CLIP
- **PaddleOCR** (50-200MB ONNX) — extract text from images
- **InsightFace** (50-500MB ONNX) — face detection + grouping by person

## Tier 3 — Powerful but heavier
- **Moondream 2** (1.5GB via Ollama) — auto-captioning
- **Florence-2** (0.7-1.5GB) — multi-task: caption + detect + OCR
- **Google Gemini Embedding 2** (API) — 3072-dim cross-modal

## Implementation Priority
1. Color palette extraction (no ML, instant)
2. AI metadata parsing (no ML, instant) 
3. pHash near-duplicate detection (no ML)
4. DINOv2 embeddings (ONNX, better clusters)
5. PaddleOCR (ONNX, text in images)
6. InsightFace (ONNX, face grouping)

title: Embedding Explorer: scope to current filter/folder/collection, not always all images
Embedding Explorer: UMAP in web worker to avoid freezing UI at 10K+ images
close_reason: Implemented UMAP projection in a web worker with cancellable projection jobs and lazy thumbnail loading.

title: Embedding Explorer: nearest-neighbor panel with similarity scores on click
Embedding Explorer: clickable clusters with representative preview thumbnails
Embedding Explorer: lasso/rectangle selection → create collection
Embedding Explorer: cluster auto-naming from tags/filenames/YOLO labels
Embedding settings panel — API keys, provider selection, model config
description:
Settings panel for embedding providers — HIDDEN BY DEFAULT.

## Default View (left panel)
- Model name: 'CLIP ViT-B/32' (just text, not dropdown)
- Stats: '87/145 images embedded'
- Buttons: 'Generate' (if images missing) or 'Up to date' (if all done)
- Gear icon (⚙) — one click opens config

## Config View (toggled by gear icon)
Shows below the stats:
- Provider dropdown
- API Key fields (only for selected provider)
- Ollama URL (only if Ollama selected)
- Download section (only if model needs download)
- 'Regenerate All' button
- Close config (⚙ again or ✕)

## Key Principle
Show only what's actionable. If embeddings are generated and model is working, the user just sees the scatter plot. Config is 1 click away but not cluttering the default view.

title: Re-run embedding generation — button to regenerate, selective re-processing
description:
Re-run embedding generation:

## Requirements
- Button to regenerate all embeddings (re-processes every image)
- Button to generate only for images without embeddings
- Progress indication during generation
- Ability to cancel mid-generation
- Status showing: N/M images have embeddings for current model
- When switching models, show which images need new embeddings

title: In-app inpainting — select region, type prompt, call API
CLI tool — headless access to import, search, export, detect
notes:
Implemented initial MCP-aligned headless CLI slice: get_library_stats, list_images, list_folders, list_collections, import_folder, import_files, list_export_presets, export_images. Remaining issue scope still includes search, detect, rate, convert, and broader CLI parity.
Refactored the headless CLI into independent domain modules under src-tauri/src/cli and added docs/agent-cli-standards.md with module ownership and CLI/MCP contract standards.

title: Collection export — export to folder with format conversion and naming template
WYSIWYG export — capture any view state at any zoom as PNG/PDF
Contact sheet export — configurable grid with labels, ratings, metadata
MCP server (stdio) — expose all app functionality to agents
Semantic similarity search — find visually similar images
notes: 2026-05-16: Added backend similarity grouping persistence and Tauri/API commands. Broader user-facing semantic search UI remains open.

title: CLIP text-to-image search — search by text description
YOLO object detection via ONNX Runtime — auto-tagging on import
description:
Object detection for auto-tagging images on import.

## Model Stack
1. **Primary: YOLO26 nano ONNX** (~6.5MB, ~10ms/image)
   - Download from Ultralytics/HuggingFace, export to ONNX
   - Run via ort crate (same as CLIP)
   - Auto-tags with 80 COCO classes (person, car, building, dog, etc.)
   - Store results in detections table (label, bbox, confidence)

2. **Secondary: Florence-2 via Ollama** (zero-shot)
   - Custom text queries: 'find images with text', 'find logos'
   - Run via Ollama HTTP API (same infrastructure as embedding providers)
   - Slower but infinitely flexible

## Architecture
- Same EmbeddingEngine pattern: download model, load via ort, run inference
- Detection results stored in existing detections table
- Tags auto-generated from top-confidence detections
- Clusters in Embedding Explorer named from most common tags

## UI
- Sidebar shows detected tags as filterable chips
- Embedding Explorer clusters named by dominant YOLO labels
- Detection bounding boxes toggleable in Loupe view

title: Browse images by folder structure — sidebar should show imported folders as tree, click to filter
description:
Currently importing a folder dumps all images into 'All Images' with no folder context. Need folder-based browsing.

## Requirements
- Sidebar shows imported folders as expandable tree (mirroring filesystem structure)
- Click a folder node to filter grid to images from that folder only
- Sub-folders shown as children in the tree
- Image count per folder shown in parentheses
- 'All Images' at top shows everything (current behavior)

## Implementation Notes
- image_files table already stores full path — can derive folder from path
- Need a Rust query: list distinct folder paths with image counts
- Frontend: tree component in Sidebar that filters images by folder prefix
- Folder tree should update after import

title: Zen mode (Shift+.) — hide all UI chrome, show only images
description:
Zen mode — hide ALL UI chrome except images.

## Trigger
- Shift+. (period) toggles zen mode on/off

## What Gets Hidden
- Tab bar
- Sidebar
- Status bar
- All borders and separators
- Window title bar area (traffic lights still accessible on hover)

## What Remains
- Images only, on pure black background
- Keyboard shortcuts still work (just no visible hints)
- Mouse hover near top edge briefly shows tab bar
- Mouse hover near bottom edge briefly shows status bar
- Escape exits zen mode

## Use Case
Distraction-free image review. Maximum screen real estate for the content.

title: Collapsible sidebar with keyboard shortcut
description:
Sidebar should be collapsible:

## Shortcuts
- Cmd+B or \ (backslash) to toggle sidebar
- When collapsed, main area expands to full width
- Sidebar state persists across sessions

## Behavior
- Smooth transition (150ms)
- In Loupe/Compare, sidebar is already hidden (immersive mode)
- This is for Grid and other modes where sidebar is shown by default

title: Grid size presets — keyboard shortcuts to toggle between thumbnail size/gap presets
description:
Keyboard-togglable presets for thumbnail size and gap between images.

## Presets (examples)
- Compact: 80px thumbs, 2px gap — maximum density
- Normal: 160px thumbs, 4px gap — default
- Large: 280px thumbs, 8px gap — review quality
- XL: 400px thumbs, 12px gap — detailed inspection

## Shortcuts
- Shift+1 through Shift+4 to toggle between presets
- Or cycle with a single key like 'g' (grid density)
- Current size slider stays as fine-tune control

## Notes
- Presets should also adjust the number visible per row
- User should be able to customize/save their own presets later

title: Compare view: arrow keys should cycle images being compared
Collections system — custom ordered sets with export to folders/formats
description:
Collections system — custom ordered sets with export to folders/formats.

## Creation Methods (all from Grid view)
1. **C key** — create collection from currently selected images
2. **Shift+C** — create from inverse (everything NOT selected / not rejected)
3. **B key** — enter Collect mode, Space adds images one by one in order, B again to finish
4. **Drag** — drag selected images to sidebar COLLECTIONS area to create/add to collection
5. **Templates** — create collection from a filter preset (e.g. 'all ★4+', 'all accepted', 'all PNG > 1024px')

## Smart Scenarios
- Select 5 in Grid → C → name → instant collection of 5
- Reject 3 with X → Shift+C → collection of everything else
- Filter ★≥4 → Cmd+A → C → collection of all high-rated
- Press B → browse and Space to pick favorites → B to finish
- Drag selection to 'New Collection' drop zone in sidebar
- Apply template 'accepted PNG > 512px' → auto-generates collection

## UI
- Collections in sidebar under PROJECTS/COLLECTIONS
- Within collection: reorderable grid with index numbers
- Drag-and-drop + Ctrl+Up/Down for keyboard reorder
- Grid view stays read-only

## Export
- Presets: web-ready, print, thumbnail-set, custom
- Template naming: {collection}/{index}_{name}.{format}
- Format conversion: PNG/JPEG/WebP with quality control
- Optional resize on export
close_reason: Implemented: schema, backend, sidebar UI, keyboard shortcuts (C, Shift+C, B collect mode)

title: Loupe view: fullscreen toggle via double-click, Enter, shortcut, Esc to exit
description:
Loupe (single image viewer) needs fullscreen support:

- Double-click thumbnail in Grid → opens Loupe fullscreen
- Enter key on focused thumbnail → opens Loupe fullscreen  
- Keyboard shortcut (e.g. F or ⌘F) → toggles fullscreen from any view
- Escape → exits fullscreen / returns to Grid
- Full window size by default when entering Loupe
- True macOS fullscreen (green button) also supported

Loupe already specified in design doc (view mode #3) with: pixel-level zoom, metadata panel, region selection, detection overlays. This issue adds the fullscreen entry/exit interactions.
close_reason: Implemented: F key and double-click toggle fullscreen in Loupe

title: Folder management - list all imported folders, delete folder (remove its images from library)
Filter images by minimum size (width/height) - brainstorm UI for filter controls in sidebar
description:
Filter images by minimum size (width/height). Brainstorm UI first.

## Interface Options

A) Sidebar filter section -- Under FILTERS section, add min-size input like 'min: 256px' with text field or slider. Simple, always visible.

B) Status bar filter chip -- Click filter icon in status bar for dropdown: 'Width >= ___ Height >= ___'. Compact, out of the way.

C) Keyboard-triggered -- Press 'f' for filter mode, type 'size>256' in command palette-style input. Most terminal-native.

D) Sidebar slider with presets -- Slider with presets: All, >64px, >256px, >512px, >1024px. One click to filter.

E) Combine D + C -- Sidebar slider for quick access, keyboard shortcut for power users.

title: Create README.md with project description, screenshots, features, build instructions, keyboard shortcuts reference
Add BSL 1.1 (Business Source License) to the repository - create LICENSE.md with BSL terms, additional use grant for non-commercial, change date 4 years out, change license Apache 2.0
Secondary text color fails WCAG AA - #565f89 on dark is 3.2:1, needs 4.5:1
Keyboard focus lost when navigating past rendered virtual grid rows
DB corruption causes crash with no recovery UI
Thumbnails have no accessible names - role=button with no label
No focus-visible styling on interactive controls
Grid capped at 10K images - rest unreachable
Grid lacks ARIA semantics - no role=grid, aria-selected, position announcements
Partial import failure leaves confusing state - image in DB before thumbnail
Space key hijacked from buttons - pressing Space on Import Folder toggles selection
Command palette: verification, accessibility, and E2E coverage
description: Add focused test coverage for command registry behavior, shortcut routing, palette filtering/ranking, keyboard interaction, and high-risk command execution paths. Include E2E coverage using the repo's Chrome Beta/agent-browser pattern once the UI shell exists.\n\nVerify desktop behavior in at least grid and loupe/compare contexts, including that normal text inputs are not hijacked by palette shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover registry filtering, shortcut conflicts, ranking/persistence helpers, and command execution wrappers; E2E covers opening the palette, searching, executing a view/navigation command, pinning or recents, and Escape/Enter/arrow behavior; svelte-check and relevant tests pass.

title: Command palette: shortcut customization and conflict handling
description: Add a settings surface for viewing and customizing command shortcuts after the registry exists. It should show default shortcuts, detect conflicts, support clearing/resetting bindings, and optionally offer an Obsidian-style Cmd+P preset with a clear warning about Print conflict on macOS.\n\nThis should also expose a searchable shortcut/help view from the palette itself.
acceptance_criteria: Users can inspect all registered shortcuts; duplicate/conflicting keybindings are detected before saving; reset-to-default works; shortcut changes affect global handling and palette badges; tests cover conflict detection and persistence.

title: Command palette: saved workflows and safe automations
description: Add user-definable workflows as saved sequences of registered Cull commands, not arbitrary scripts. Start with parameterized workflows for common curation actions such as review a new import batch, create collection from selection, export current scope, run detection/analyze remaining, and prepare delivery set.\n\nThis depends on the shared registry so workflows can reuse command IDs, context predicates, confirmations, and permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Users can save, rename, delete, pin, and run at least one workflow made from known command IDs; workflow steps validate required context before running; destructive or expensive steps request confirmation; failures stop with a clear toast/error state and do not leave partial UI state hidden.

title: Command palette: ranking, recents, and pinned items
description: Add ranking behavior that makes the palette feel fast for daily work: pinned items first, then recent/frequent/context-relevant items, then fuzzy matches. Persist pinned command/search/workflow IDs and recent usage safely.\n\nPinning must distinguish palette pins from the existing pinnedCollection import target. Consider separate labels such as Favorite command or Pinned palette item rather than reusing collection pin language in UI copy.
acceptance_criteria: Palette supports pin/unpin for commands and dynamic destinations where stable IDs exist; recent/frequent usage changes ranking; persisted data survives restart; missing/stale pinned destinations are handled without crashes; UI copy avoids confusing palette pins with pinned import collection.

title: Best-of-group ranking plan
description: Define and implement explainable best-of-group ranking using user rating/decision, quality metrics, similarity group rank, and override metadata. Do not auto-delete or auto-select without user confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: Each group exposes a suggested winner, score components, and explicit user override support.

title: Client feedback and delivery lists
description: Add client favorites, comments, CSV/export lists, and proof/final delivery set workflow on top of collections.
acceptance_criteria: Client feedback is stored separately from local curator selections and export can produce CSV/list outputs.

title: Fix pagination clamp test mismatch
description: Full Rust test suite currently fails in services::library::tests::test_pagination_clamped because Pagination::clamped returns 200 for limit=200 while the test expects 100. Local workspace already has a change raising the clamp in src-tauri/src/services/mod.rs from 100 to 250; resolve by deciding intended max limit and updating implementation or test consistently.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes without changing unrelated behavior.
close_reason: Updated pagination clamp expectation to the new 250-item page cap and verified cargo test passes.

title: Track local asset load failures
description: Persist privacy-safe local diagnostics when image assets fail to load in UI views, starting with loupe source images and thumbnails.
acceptance_criteria: A local SQLite event log records failed image asset loads without storing full paths; loupe falls back to thumbnails when source loads fail; commands/API can record and query recent events; focused tests cover persistence and event shaping.
close_reason: Implemented local asset load event logging, loupe thumbnail fallback, Tauri commands/API, and focused coverage.

title: Capture One-style bulk rename patterns
description: Add a bulk rename workflow inspired by Capture One naming patterns. Support tokenized templates for original name, sequence/counter, date/time, folder/session/collection, rating/decision, provider/model where available, and literal text. Preserve extensions by default, preview every target filename before applying, detect collisions, and integrate with undo.
acceptance_criteria: Grid or batch workflow can preview a rename template before applying; sequence counters are deterministic; extensions are preserved unless explicitly changed; collisions are blocked with clear per-file errors; applied renames update image_files paths and grid state.

title: P2: Print support — print current image or contact sheet with layout options
P2: Color management — ICC profile awareness, monitor profile matching for accurate color display
P2: Services menu integration — NSServices in Info.plist for 'Open in ImageView' in system-wide right-click menu
Basic image stats on import — aspect ratio, orientation, transparency, bit depth
PaddleOCR via ONNX — extract text from images for search
description:
PaddleOCR via ONNX — extract text from images.

## Models  
- Detection model: ~5MB ONNX
- Recognition model: ~50-200MB ONNX (per language)
- Total: ~60-210MB

## What it adds
- Extract text overlays, titles, watermarks from AI-generated images
- Searchable text content
- Filter 'images containing text'

## Source: huggingface.co/monkt/paddleocr-onnx

title: Color palette extraction — k-means dominant colors, no ML needed
description:
Extract 5 dominant colors via k-means on import. No ML model needed.

## Schema: image_palette_colors table
- Columns: image_id, algorithm, rank (0-4), r, g, b, hex, population (%), hue/sat/light buckets
- Indexed by HSL buckets for 'find red images' queries
- ~20ms per image after decode (downsample to 128x128 first)

## Crates
- kmeans_colors — k-means palette extraction
- palette — RGB/Lab/HSL conversion
- Downsample with image crate before k-means

## UI
- Color swatches shown in Grid overlay and Sidebar filter
- Filter by dominant color in sidebar
- Sort by color similarity

comments.author: glebis
comments.text: Backend/import slice landed in the current tree: schema v19 image_color_metrics, local color analyzer, import-time metrics, smart-collection color fields, Tauri/API commands, mocks, and Rust tests. Remaining issue scope: grid/sidebar swatches, visible color filter UI, and color-similarity sort.

title: DINOv2 embeddings as alternative to CLIP — better visual similarity
description:
DINOv2 embeddings as CLIP alternative. 384/768-dim, better for visual similarity clustering.

## Models
- ViT-S/14: 384-dim, ~300MB ONNX, fastest
- ViT-B/14: 768-dim, ~600MB ONNX, better quality
- ViT-L/14: 1024-dim, ~1.1GB ONNX, best quality

## Why
- Self-supervised (no text training) → better at pure visual similarity
- Dense descriptors → better segmentation-aware features
- ONNX available: github.com/sefaburakokcu/dinov2_onnx

## DB: 384-768 dims × 4 bytes = 1.5-3KB per image
close_reason: Implemented DINOv2 ViT-S/14 ONNX embedding support via shared embedding model specs, Tauri/API commands, headless CLI, and MCP tools; downloaded the model and generated 24 real-library 384-dim embeddings with zero failures.

title: InsightFace via ONNX — face detection and grouping by person
description:
InsightFace via ONNX — face detection and grouping by person.

## Models
- SCRFD detector: ~2.5-5MB ONNX (face detection + landmarks)
- ArcFace recognizer: ~50-500MB ONNX (face embeddings, 512-dim)
- Total: ~55-505MB depending on accuracy tier

## What it adds
- Detect faces in images, store bbox + landmarks
- Generate face embeddings for grouping by person
- 'Show all images of person X' feature
- Face count as metadata for filtering

## Source: github.com/deepinsight/insightface

title: Generate mockups for Embedding Explorer settings and download UI
Lineage view — iteration tree visualization with prompt diffs
AppleScript support — tell app to get selected images, etc
Canvas view — infinite spatial canvas with freeform image placement and grouping
Global keyboard shortcuts — system-wide hotkeys for toggle, clipboard import
Tab bar icons — unique icon for each view mode (grid, compare, loupe, canvas, lineage, embeddings, export)
close_reason: Added Unicode icons to all 7 tabs

title: Create CHANGELOG.md with initial v0.1.0 entry
Create CONTRIBUTING.md with development setup, code style, PR process
Thumbnail size slider has no aria-label
No broken image fallback - failed thumbnail/image load is invisible
Missing Home/End/PageUp/PageDown shortcuts, no way to clear rating
Import progress not announced for screen readers - no aria-live
Star rating and accept/reject only discoverable via status bar hints
Make native menus state-aware
description: Follow-up from the app menu expansion: convert view/toggle menu items to state-aware checked or enabled states where Tauri supports it, and audit shortcut conflicts such as app undo/redo versus text-field undo. Current implementation exposes the actions, but menu state is static.
acceptance_criteria: View mode and toggle menu items reflect current state; unavailable actions are disabled where possible; app-level undo/redo remains discoverable without breaking text-field editing.

title: AI-assisted rename suggestions for image batches
description: Add optional AI-generated filename suggestions for selected images after the deterministic bulk rename pattern workflow exists. Suggestions should use image metadata, prompts, provider/model tags, and optional vision captions, then require user review before any filesystem rename.
acceptance_criteria: AI suggestions are preview-only by default; user can accept/edit/reject each suggestion; no files are renamed without explicit confirmation; prompts and image data usage are visible in the privacy/audit surfaces; deterministic pattern rename remains available without AI.

title: Add .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE and PR template
